clues: The name of this disease comes from an ancient Greek word meaning 'a short draped military cloak' because the bacteria cloak the nucleus of the host cells it infects
This is an obligate intracellular bacteria and causes a disease which is actually a massive public health threat to Australian koalas
Although typically thought of as affecting the reproductive system, some strains can also affect the eye. This devastating version is the world's leading preventable cause of blindness.

Due to concerns about cardiovascular safety, other medications in the same class were pulled from the market. The PRECISION trial helped establish safety of this medication regarding cardiovascular events.
Beyond treating pain, this medication is also approved for the treatment of familial adenomatous polyposis
Not available over the counter, this medication was designed to target inflammation with fewer GI side effects
Although better known for his influence on psychotherapy, in the 1880s Sigmund Freud heavily promoted this as a miracle cure for morphine addiction.
One of few drugs that combines numbing and blood vessel constricting properties, this is used occasionally in ENT surgeries involving nasal passages
Ironically, intranasal use of this can also lead to significant tissue damage and a perforated septum
This devastating disease is suspected to be responsible for the Plague of Athens in 430 BC where it killed up to a third of the city's population including the leader at the time, Pericles
Only carried by humans, this disease causes fever, relative bradycardia, and characteristic flat, light-pink spots on patients' chest and abdomen

Some individuals may become chronic, asymptomatic carriers of this disease where bacteria colonize the gallbladder, liver, or bile ducts and can be shed in feces or urine. The most famous example is Mary Mallon

In medieval times, this disease was believed to be caused by a wolf's bite. Because the lesions were believed to be eating the flesh, the earliest recorded treatment was applying freshly killed chicken flesh to the lesions so the disease would devour the chicken instead of the patient

Around this time, the associated extreme photosensitivity led to myths that patients were turning into werewolves. Because sunlight triggered skin rashes and flares, affected individuals were often forced to stay indoors during the day, emerging only at night
Because of these beliefs, the name is derived from the Latin word for wolf. Selena Gomez also has this disease which led to kidney failure and a transplant in 2017
